mnemonics: F.A.S.T. Revolution: Fossil fuels, Automation, Steam power, Textiles. These four pillars FAST-tracked society into the industrial age and its massive environmental impact, which Green IT now aims to reverse.
keyPoints: The Industrial Revolution marked a shift from agrarian societies to industrialized ones, powered by fossil fuels like coal, which began the era of significant anthropogenic carbon emissions.
It introduced mass production and mechanization, drastically increasing resource consumption and waste, setting a precedent for our linear 'take-make-dispose' economy.
This period is the historical foundation for the modern environmental challenges (climate change, pollution, resource depletion) that Green IT and sustainability principles seek to address.
commonMistakes: Thinking the Industrial Revolution is irrelevant to IT. Modern data centers and tech manufacturing are direct descendants of its industrial legacy and energy demands.
Believing sustainability is only about future tech. A core principle is understanding the historical resource-intensive systems we must move away from, which originated in this era.
